answer: The discharge temperature reaches 125°F with suction pressure at 70 psig.
Suction pressure of 75 psig is within normal range. High discharge pressure of 275 psig.
The system is running at 999°F which is dangerously high.
In cold climates the ambient may reach -10°C with pressures around -5 psig.
The technician noted the superheat was 65 degrees above saturation.
